what_it_does: A single JSON ledger that defines the completeness and quality contract for the design corpus. It enumerates real application features, maps them to design views and user-journey sequences, records which mockup variants have been produced, and states the quality bar and status flow that each artifact must satisfy.
observations: It is the merged successor to manifest.json, retired to _archive/.
It lists 60+ features with area, code references, and optional build_required/proposed/built flags.
It lists ~41 design views grouped into areas A–G, each with platforms, represents[] feature ids, journeys, and status.
It defines an 8-item quality_bar (GROUNDED, LINKED, ON-SYSTEM, RESPONSIVE, JOURNEY-MOMENT, UX, SELF-DESCRIBING).
It defines a status flow: planned -> drafting -> drafted -> quality-passed -> approved(Tim).
It defines 9 user-journey sequences with steps referencing view ids, spine flags, storyboard_status, and surfaces_when.
The coverage map is only a placeholder comment; it is described as auto-derivable from views[].represents.
produced.wave1 contains 17 view-variants with file/thumb paths and reconciliation notes that downgrade earlier over-claimed statuses to match views[].status.
Several quality-passed views carry notes that explicitly split EXISTING real behavior from PROPOSED design treatment to avoid fiction.
Some features are marked built but do not appear in any view's represents[] array.
standouts: It acts as a completeness contract: every feature must have >=1 view, every view must exist in each platform variant, every spine sequence must have a storyboard, every mockup must pass the quality_bar.
It contains an explicit authority rule: views[].status is the truth and produced[].status is reconciled down to it, because produced is a build log, not a quality verdict.
The quality bar foregrounds groundedness: no fictional UI, build-required items labelled proposed, and real code refs required.
produced.wave1 includes narrative D-1 reconciliation notes correcting prior over-claims of quality-passed.
conventions: Uses _prefix for meta fields (_what, _traceability) and _comment for inline documentation.
Uses file:line-grounded code references and ui:// URIs for proposed surfaces.
Mixes structured data and explanatory narrative inside the same string fields.
concerns: coverage is an empty placeholder despite being described as the reverse index the loop checks; it cannot actually be verified until populated.
produced statuses were historically over-claimed and are now manually reconciled, creating ongoing drift risk between the build log and view statuses.
Several features marked built (e.g. INB-propose, NODE-model, RHM-utterance, WIRE-resolve) are not represented in any view's represents[], leaving coverage gaps.
Some proposed features have empty code fields, which is honest but makes their grounding harder to verify from this file alone.
build_required is duplicated on both features and views, which could become inconsistent.
notes: This is the design corpus's single source of truth and completeness ledger: it ties real code features to views, journeys, and produced mockups, and enforces a grounded, quality-barred status workflow.
questions: What process or script performs the running audit that populates coverage and verifies represents[]?
How is the coverage reverse index derived and kept in sync with views[].represents in practice?
What is the exact gating for the final approved(Tim) status?
Why do some features marked built have no representing view, and is that intentional?
How are proposed_at ui:// URIs intended to map onto views or journeys?
How are mobile variants validated against the RESPONSIVE quality-bar criteria?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to be the single production register and completeness/quality contract for the design corpus. It largely fulfills this, but the coverage reverse index is still a placeholder and produced statuses required manual reconciliation to views[].status, so the build log is not yet independently authoritative.
apparent_themes: Grounded design linked to real code
Traceability between features, views, and journeys
Honest labelling of proposed versus existing functionality
intention_signals: It is trying to make the design process auditable and machine-checkable: an autonomous loop should be able to verify that every real feature is visually represented, every view meets the quality bar, and no produced mockup over-claims implementation status.
novelty: The explicit groundedness quality bar and the authority rule that views[].status overrides produced[].status to prevent build logs from falsely claiming quality-passed.
contribution: A structured, code-grounded design register that connects features, views, journeys, and produced mockup variants with a quality contract; it provides a machine-checkable completeness target and a precedent for honest status reconciliation.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON register that maps real application features to design views, user-journey sequences, and produced mockup variants, while defining a groundedness quality bar and a status workflow for design artifacts.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single source of truth and completeness contract for the design corpus, enabling an autonomous mockup loop to verify that every real feature is visually represented and quality-passed.
